design_evolution_notes.description: Radiator 1 is the starting design: a microstrip line-fed triangular patch with a complete ground plane.
design_evolution_notes.label: Reference radiator

design_evolution_notes.description: Radiator 2 adds a 1 mm elliptical slot at the patch center as an intermediate compactness step.
design_evolution_notes.label: Elliptical-slot iteration

design_evolution_notes.description: Radiator 3 reuses the optimized prior geometry and inserts a 2 mm × 5 mm rectangular slot for further miniaturization.
design_evolution_notes.label: Rectangular-slot iteration

design_evolution_notes.description: Radiator 4 inserts a central 3 mm circular slot and is explicitly identified as the best choice for the 5.8 GHz ISM band.
design_evolution_notes.label: Final circular-slot iteration

design_evolution_notes.description: The paper compares all four radiator variants in simulated results; no separate measurement plot is retrieved in the provided evidence.
design_evolution_notes.label: Simulation comparison set

identified_antennas: Radiator 1: microstrip line-fed triangular patch radiator with full ground plane
Radiator 2: triangular patch with a 1 mm elliptical slot
Radiator 3: triangular patch with a 2 mm × 5 mm rectangular slot
Radiator 4: circularly slotted triangular patch radiator with a 3 mm radius central slot
Proposed CSTPA: the same final design as Radiator 4
Figure 1a–d parametric variants of the CSTPA
proposed_final_antenna_rationale: Radiator 4 is the paper’s dominant proposed antenna because the authors explicitly call the Step 4 circular-slot geometry the best choice for 5.8 GHz ISM-band use, and the design caption ties Figure 1d to the suggested CSTPA geometry. The earlier elliptical-slot and rectangular-slot versions are presented as intermediate optimization stages; they are compared in Table 2 but are not presented as the final selected topology. The final design therefore is the circularly slotted triangular patch with a 3 mm central slot radius, not the earlier Radiator 2 or Radiator 3 variants.
selected_design_rationale: The paper explicitly labels Step 4 / Radiator 4 as the suggested geometry and states that the circular-slot-based triangular patch is the best choice for ISM-band operation. Step 2 (elliptical slot) and Step 3 (rectangular slot) are intermediate miniaturization variants, while the final narrative and figure captions center on the 3 mm-radius circular slot variant.
selected_design_summary: The dominant final design is Radiator 4 / Step 4: a microstrip line-fed circularly slotted triangular patch antenna (CSTPA) for the 5.8 GHz ISM band, implemented on FR4 with a 3 mm central circular slot and a reduced 25.5 × 22.5 × 1.6 mm footprint.

unresolved_conflicts.description: The abstract and conclusion claim a 100 MHz bandwidth at 5.8 GHz with 3% FBW, but Table 2 and Table 3 report different final-step numerical spans for the proposed design (e.g. 5.72–5.88 GHz or 5.70–5.88 GHz) and a gain value of 1.49 dBi. The paper’s narrative favors the circular-slot final design, but the exact reported bandwidth figure is inconsistent across sections.

The retrieved evidence repeatedly refers to simulated results and simulated tables/figures, but no explicit fabricated prototype or measured validation results are present. The conclusion says the antenna was 'designed and developed,' which does not resolve whether hardware measurements were taken.

The abstract mentions changes in the practical radius of the triangular patch and the main-axis radius of the circular slot, but Table 1 only explicitly enumerates patch (x,y), slot radius, feedline, and ground dimensions. The exact mapping of those descriptive terms to the final geometry is not fully explicit in the retrieved text.
unresolved_conflicts.topic: Mapping of abstracted geometric terms to final dimensions
